title: Pre-Qualification Requirements and Procedures for Licensing of Recruitment Agencies
content:
SECTION 4. Pre-Qualification Requirements: Any person applying for a license to operate a recruitment agency must file a written application with the Administration along with specified documents including business registration certificates, proof of financial capacity (e.g., bank certificate with deposit of at least PhP5,000,000, income tax returns, proof of investment sources), proof of existence of new market (e.g., POLO verified recruitment agreements, employer profiles), proof of educational or experience qualifications of key officers, valid clearances from NBI and Anti-Illegal Recruitment Branch, notarized undertakings regarding ethical recruitment and liabilities, list of officials and personnel with clearances, certificate of attendance in Pre-Licensing Orientation Seminar, recruitment procedure flowchart, and a four-year business plan.

SECTION 5. Payment of Filing Fee: Upon receipt of complete application requirements, a non-refundable filing fee of PhP25,000 is required.

SECTION 6. Panel Interview: Proprietor, partners, and board members undergo a panel interview to verify qualifications and compliance.

SECTION 7. Decision on Application: The Administration shall notify the applicant in writing of the decision within three working days after the interview.

SECTION 8. Grounds for Denial: Applications may be denied for failure to meet requirements or submission of false information or documents.

SECTION 9. Submission of Post-Qualification Requirements: Prior to issuance of provisional license, applicants must submit lease contract or proof of office ownership (minimum 100 sqm), office layout, inventory of office equipment and facilities, organizational chart, payment of license fee of PhP100,000, and an escrow agreement with a bank with a deposit of PhP1,000,000 to cover claims and compliance guarantees.

SECTION 10. Assessment Prior to Issuance of License: The Administration shall assess compliance with post-qualification requirements including office size and facilities, conducted by the Labor Laws Compliance Officer as per Rule III, Part II of these Rules.
